column10: Active counter equals accepted non-terminal executions; no orphan job.

column3: [TL:integration] [FLOW-002][SIT] [EXC-STACK-001] OpenAPI, dependency-boundary and supported-version gates refute brownfield stack drift
column4:
- Approved API-001–023 schemas and prior consumer fixtures are available.
- PR-008 boundary fixtures include allowed domain/application imports and forbidden Fastify, Prisma, provider HTTP client, Pino, Sentry, Vault SDK and Vue/browser imports from domain/application modules.
- Repository manifests declare Node, Fastify and Vue versions; TG-27 supplies a versioned support/EOL metadata snapshot and the Architecture baseline Node >=20.20, Fastify 5 and Vue 3.
column5: Approved OpenAPI/error schemas; additive/breaking mutations; PR-008 allowed and forbidden import fixtures; repository runtime/dependency manifests; TG-27 pinned support/EOL metadata snapshot; no production PII.

- Verify additive optional change and injected remove/rename/retype/error-code mutations.
- Run the dependency-boundary scanner over allowed and one-at-a-time forbidden imports.

column3: [TL:integration] [FLOW-003][SIT] Dependency timeouts, circuit breakers and bulkheads fail closed at exact thresholds
column4:
- Each dependency fake can emit 4xx, 5xx, timeout, partition and malformed response without reading real credentials.
- Threshold config is fixed to IAM status 2s, login 5s with max 3 attempts, key provider 2s, provider 30s, circuit 50%/30s/open 60s, provider bulkhead 5 per Host and DB pool 20 per process.
- Non-provider retry fixtures cover Central-IAM login/token exchange, Secret Manager safe key read, MySQL idempotent read/lease claim and Telemetry exporter; each uses an injected deterministic full-jitter source with base=100ms, cap=1000ms and candidate delay rows 0/50/100ms.
column5: Synthetic identity/secret markers; crashable transaction; two Hosts for isolation.
column6:
- Exercise IAM status timeout and login retries; missing/invalid/stale signed manifest; key timeout; database rollback; provider 4xx/5xx/30s timeout/partition.
- Drive exactly 49% then 50% failures in 30s, probe at 59s/60s open duration, saturate 5/6 provider slots for one Host and 20/21 DB connections.
- For each retry-safe non-provider operation, inject transient failures through three total attempts and record scheduled delays; also invoke an unclassified mutation and IAM per-request status check.
column7:
- [ ] IAM status timeout returns typed 503 within 2s; login performs at most 3 attempts of 5s; manifest/key uncertainty fails closed before mutation or credential send.
- [ ] Database failure rolls back all rows; provider failures never commit mixed snapshots or plaintext credential material.
- [ ] Circuit remains closed at 49%, opens at 50% for 60s; sixth same-Host provider call and twenty-first DB checkout are rejected/queued without starving the unrelated Host.
- [ ] Every retry-safe non-provider operation performs at most three total attempts; each deterministic delay falls within `0..min(1000ms,100ms*2^(attempt-1))` and matches the injected full-jitter value. The unclassified mutation and IAM per-request status check execute once with no retry.

column3: [TL:integration] [FLOW-002][SIT] Durable job, lease heartbeat and recovery remain non-blocking and exactly-once
column4:
- A committed run job, controllable 60-second lease/15-second heartbeat and crashable worker.
- The execution owns one active-capacity slot; recovery claims are numbered; an authorized operations actor and an unauthorized authenticated actor are available; inspect evidence includes job ID, Execution ID, claim count, lease/heartbeat timestamps and masked terminal error.
column5: Synthetic deterministic fixtures; isolated ephemeral schema; no production PII.
column6: - Crash the claimant before and after provider evidence persistence, run recovery through the third exhausted claim, query through the exact TG-13 runbook-defined inspection command/API, then invoke its manual-recovery operation first as the unauthorized actor and then as the authorized operations actor.
column7: Run returns only after execution and job commit, without waiting for provider completion.
column10: Worker heartbeat/reclaim produces one terminal outcome and releases capacity.
column17: N/A — durable queue, lease and recovery behavior has no direct interactive surface

column3: [TL:integration] [NFR-004][Security+SIT] [EXC-AUTH-001] OIDC callback state, PKCE, MFA and protected paths enforce one fail-closed browser boundary
column4:
- Active, expired, revoked, inactive and IAM-uncertain synthetic identities; valid CSRF only for active actor.
- API-017 creates one server-side state record bound to return URL and a PKCE S256 challenge. Callback rows include correct, missing, mismatched, expired and replayed state; correct/missing/wrong verifier; and MFA-sufficient/insufficient `amr`/`acr` claims.
column5: Synthetic active/expired/revoked/uncertain identities; one-time state records; PKCE verifier/challenge pairs; MFA-sufficient/insufficient `amr`/`acr` claims; isolated ephemeral schema; no production PII.
column6: - Execute API-017→API-018 for every state/PKCE/MFA row, replay the successful callback, then invoke protected actions through UI and direct API including method/path tampering.
column7: Unauthenticated, expired, revoked and uncertain sessions are denied and return URL is preserved where allowed.
column10: No protected handler mutates or returns payload when identity enforcement rejects.
column17: SCREEN-001–008 protected routes and workspace surfaces; this case verifies their shared authentication boundary, while TC-072 owns exact visual-state observation

column3: [TL:integration] [NFR-005][SIT] Backup, PITR and restore satisfy RPO/RTO and atomic durability
column4: - Encrypted backup/binlog chain and synthetic dataset with committed/uncommitted transaction markers.

column3: [TL:integration] [NFR-006][SIT] Correlation, audit, alerts and exporter degradation meet exact observability bounds
column4:
- Alert fixtures cover job oldest age at 60s and >60s sustained 5m; dead jobs 0/1; retention lag 48h/>48h; error rate 5%/>5% sustained 5m; active-count invariant match/mismatch.
- Audit clock covers online 6 months and retained 12 months; exporter is unavailable for 24h.
- Ingress fixtures send a missing, valid 64-character, overlength 65-character, malformed and duplicate `X-Request-ID`, plus a trusted generated and direct untrusted `X-Trace-ID`; the flow crosses response, downstream HTTP, job and worker boundaries.
- Offline Sentry fixtures enqueue 100 and 101 redacted envelopes aged 23h59m59s, 24h and 24h+1s; envelope variants include or omit `app_version`, immutable `build_id`, session ID, masked user ID, device/OS/browser/viewport/network class and 10 redacted breadcrumbs.
- Release fixtures contain a verified source-map upload or a missing upload, remove uploaded maps from the public bundle, and keep the upload credential absent from runtime/browser configuration.
column5: Known 64-character request ID, trace ID, execution ID and synthetic secret markers.
column6:
- Run success/failure flows, disable exporter, drive every alert threshold at below/equal/above values and advance audit clock.
- Exercise every request/trace-ID vector and inspect the response echo, downstream HTTP headers, job payload, worker context and structured logs; verify neither identifier participates in authentication or idempotency decisions.
- Enqueue offline envelopes at the 100/101 boundary, advance the controllable clock through the TTL boundary, reconnect, emit each approved frontend error category and LCP/INP/CLS, and inspect the local breadcrumb ring.
- Query by request/trace/execution ID immediately and after 5 minutes; execute the source-map promotion gate and scan envelopes, bundles and runtime configuration for PII/secrets.
column7:
- [ ] A valid request ID of ≤64 characters is retained; missing, malformed, duplicate or 65-character values are replaced by one server-generated ID; direct untrusted trace IDs are replaced by the trusted telemetry context.
- [ ] Responses echo the accepted/generated request and trace IDs; downstream HTTP, job, worker, provider and structured-log evidence propagates the same pair and is searchable within 5 minutes, without using either identifier for authorization or idempotency.
- [ ] Structured logs include `timestamp,level,service,environment,trace_id,request_id,user_id,operation,message,duration_ms,http_status,error_code,error_message`; correlation coverage is 100%.
- [ ] Alerts do not fire at exact strict boundaries; they fire for oldest job >60s sustained 5m, dead job >0, retention lag >48h, error rate >5% sustained 5m and any active-count invariant mismatch.
- [ ] Offline queue size 100 is accepted; the 101st envelope is dropped with an overflow metric; an item at 23h59m59s retries on reconnect, while items aged ≥24h expire and increment the expiry/drop metric without rolling back business data.
- [ ] Every accepted envelope has the required app/build/session/masked-user/device/OS/browser/viewport/network context and at most the latest 10 redacted breadcrumbs; `sendDefaultPii=false`, raw secret/body/email/phone scans are zero, and LCP/INP/CLS plus approved error categories are emitted.
- [ ] Promotion blocks if verified source maps are not uploaded; after upload maps are absent from the public bundle and upload credentials are absent from runtime/browser configuration.
- [ ] Audit stays append-only, online 6 months and retained 12 months; secret scan remains zero.

column3: [TL:integration] [NFR-007][BVA+ST] Retention, 200 KiB payload and exact 10-second Undo boundaries
column4: - Controllable clock; artifacts at 29d23:59:59, 30d, >30d; masked payloads 204799/204800/204801 bytes; API tombstone.
column5: Synthetic deterministic fixtures; isolated ephemeral schema; no production PII.
column6: - Run cleanup batches and Undo at 9.999s, 10.000s and 10.001s.
column7:
- [ ] Exact 30-day records remain and older records delete in batches <=5,000.
- [ ] Payload <=204800 persists fully; 204801 persists bounded prefix/digest/count with truncated=true.
- [ ] Only server-authorized within-window Undo restores same identity; dependent workflows remain DISABLED.

column3: [TL:e2e] [FLOW-002][SIT] [RISK-OPEN-001][Performance] Same-task AS-IS baseline and Workflow evidence prove the Product time and copy/paste KPIs
column4:
- Protocol `tc076-v1`, its canonical marker-delimited SHA-256, next attempt sequence, pilot/device identity, exact three requests/responses, allowed actions and exclusion enum from the TC-076 Protocol Contract are frozen before either run; TG-24/TG-27/TG-30 independently reproduce the digest with the specified raw-byte algorithm.
- Provider health/warm-up and initial reset pass; AS-IS starts at the first API-1 Run and ends at exact API-3 success. Workflow time uses canonical ENT-007 creation to the first matching ENT-011 success.
- A copy/paste event is one user-initiated transfer of an API output value into a later API input. The baseline count must be an observed integer >0; clipboard content is never recorded.
column5: Exact `CUST-001`/`SKU-001`/`ORD-001` provider fixture, three requests/responses, allowed actions, expected final output and protocol source/hash from the TC-076 Protocol Contract revision `tc076-v1`; pilot `khanh-pham`; no real credential/PII or payload/clipboard content in evidence.
column6:
- The same pilot executes the exact AS-IS allowed actions, the protocol reset, then the exact Workflow allowed actions; observer records status/reason before calculation.
- TG-27 correlates the observation with canonical ENT-007/011 fields and NFR-006 telemetry. TG-30 scans the union of evidence roots, verifies `current.json`, and calculates `elapsed_seconds=(finished_at-created_at)/1000` plus `reduction_pct=((baseline_copy_paste-workflow_copy_paste)/baseline_copy_paste)*100`.
column7:
- [ ] Observation includes unique sequence/set ID, exact protocol source/hash, fixture/device fields, `VALID|EXCLUDED`, exclusion enum/note, non-null AS-IS timestamps, Workflow/Execution IDs and integer counts without sensitive payload.
- [ ] Correlation selects `ENT-007.created_at` and the smallest matching successful `(ENT-011.finished_at, ENT-011.id)` using UTC millisecond precision; tracking ID/status/timestamp mismatch yields `KPI_INCONCLUSIVE`.
- [ ] Missing evidence, baseline copy/paste <=0, task/cohort/protocol/device mismatch, an `EXCLUDED`/invalid/incomplete attempt or telemetry mismatch yields `KPI_INCONCLUSIVE`; a `VALID` non-excluded attempt with complete matching evidence that misses either KPI yields `FAILED`; only a `VALID` attempt meeting both targets yields `PASSED`.
- [ ] Workflow elapsed time from creation to first successful three-Step Execution is <=10 minutes.
- [ ] Copy/paste reduction is >=80% using the observed AS-IS denominator; the report records baseline count, Workflow count, formula result and linked redacted evidence.
- [ ] TG-30 emits immutable decision plus atomic `current.json`; selector equals the greatest `tc076-v1` sequence across all roots and all paths/hashes/set IDs match. `PASSED` requires every check and target; valid miss is `FAILED`; invalid/incomplete/mismatch is `KPI_INCONCLUSIVE`; newest observation without decision is `PENDING`.
